biteProtocol.phase3_reEncryption: live on testnet (verified by us via direct precompile probe)
biteProtocol.criticalFinding: BITE precompiles require evm_version='istanbul' as the SKALE programmable-privacy skill specifies. Compiling with evm_version='cancun' produces bytecode that cannot successfully invoke 0x1B/0x1C/0x1D - the calls OOG without returning. Failed tx 0x6abf4c4282bbb2e700bca921e1a3d3feddeb5d415987c73897fec1b25f10ac4c was caller compiled for cancun. After switching to istanbul, precompile invocation works as documented.
biteProtocol.secondCriticalFinding: EncryptECIES validates the public key is on the secp256k1 curve. Passing arbitrary (x, y) bytes32 pairs causes OOG. Failed tx 0x42fb708aa7d6fc30517a370ee3f63505a513c2c3a5db57441888d4d07849ef6f used invalid (0x...01, 0x...02). Real secp256k1 points work.

phaseIntegration.phase3_ecies: BITE.encryptECIES(0x1C) inside onDecrypt to re-encrypt each winner's payout under their viewer key
phaseIntegration.phase3_te: TE encryption performed client-side via bite-ts before submitSealedBet

config.ctxCallbackValueFormatted: 0.002 CREDIT (testnet sizing; mainnet target 0.06 ETH/SFUEL per SKALE skill)

v2_cancun.reason: Compiled with evm_version=cancun which is incompatible with BITE precompiles on SKALE Base Sepolia. Replaced by v2-istanbul above.

v0_cancun.reason: Initial single-encryption deployment with incorrect callbackFee (1000 gwei). Replaced by v2-istanbul above.

PrecompileSmoke_diagnostic.purpose: Diagnostic contract that exposes BITE.encryptTE and BITE.encryptECIES via thin wrappers. Used to verify precompiles are live. Can be reused for future probes.

developmentStubs.note: Auxiliary contracts. Not used by the production v2-istanbul contract.
